product_type: Software as a Medical Device (SaMD), AI-based clinical decision support system
brief_description: A cloud-based AI system that analyzes wearable device data to detect early signs of cardiovascular disease and provides risk scores and visual insights for clinicians.
intended_purpose: To support clinicians by identifying early signs of cardiovascular disease from wearable device data and presenting risk scores and visual insights; it does not autonomously diagnose or decide treatment.
core_technologies: AI/ML-based analysis, risk scoring, visual analytics
data_processing_type: Analysis of wearable data to detect early signs of cardiovascular disease and generate risk scores and visual insights
safety_risks: Potential for incorrect or misleading risk scores or visual insights that could affect clinical decision-making; risk of overreliance despite human-in-the-loop use
cybersecurity_risks: Cloud-based system may be exposed to unauthorized access, data breaches, or tampering with clinical data or outputs
privacy_risks: Processing of wearable device data may involve sensitive health data and create risks of unauthorized disclosure or misuse
human_in_the_loop: Yes; the system supports clinicians and does not make autonomous diagnoses or treatment decisions
regulated_domains: Medical devices, healthcare, AI/ML-enabled clinical decision support, health data privacy and security
possible_classification: Software as a Medical Device (SaMD); likely regulated medical device subject to clinical evaluation, risk management, cybersecurity, and post-market surveillance requirements
lifecycle_stages: Development, clinical validation, deployment, monitoring, post-market surveillance
missing_information: Specific jurisdiction(s) and applicable regulatory framework; intended patient population; exact cardiovascular conditions targeted; device/software interoperability and integrations; data retention and governance details; model training, validation, and update/change management approach; performance metrics; clinical evidence plan; cybersecurity controls; privacy/legal basis and consent model; quality management system; risk management process; post-market monitoring procedures; whether outputs are intended for diagnosis, screening, triage, or monitoring; whether the system is standalone or integrated into another clinical workflow
